caseMetadata.caseName: State of Oregon v. Taylor Durden
caseMetadata.court: Circuit Court of the State of Oregon, Chinook County

charges.elements: Recklessly causing the death of Jessica Bateson during the commission of hazing[cite: 157, 159].
Intentionally compelling a victim to engage in acts with unreasonable risk of harm as a condition of membership[cite: 161, 163].

stipulations: The death of Jessica Bateson occurred on August 25, 2018[cite: 184].
Defendant Taylor Durden was over age 21 at the time of the incident[cite: 185].
Carmen Cordova and the victim's brother, Colt Bateson, are unavailable (deceased/deployed)[cite: 186, 187].
All 14 exhibits are authentic; chain of custody and signatures are not in dispute[cite: 189, 190, 191].
Consent of the person hazed is NOT a defense[cite: 313, 2386].

Sgt. Chris Knight.notes: 13-year veteran. Former ESE pledge rejected for alleged cheating[cite: 376, 388, 389]. Initially saw no criminal conduct until the autopsy ruling[cite: 418, 458].
Sgt. Chris Knight.crossExam: Grudge against ESE; failed to secure the scene immediately[cite: 389, 466].

Dr. Jaden Chessler.notes: Deputy State Medical Examiner. Board certified in anatomical pathology[cite: 578, 579]. Ruled death a homicide via acute hyponatremia[cite: 593, 628].
Dr. Jaden Chessler.crossExam: Lack of experience (first hyponatremia case); personal bias against Dr. Jackson[cite: 598, 633, 634].

Alex Richards.notes: 4.0 GPA senior. Called 911[cite: 713, 716]. Granted immunity in exchange for testimony[cite: 469, 786]. Claims Durden pushed pledges too far[cite: 739].
Alex Richards.crossExam: Testifying to avoid personal liability; sanctioned similar games as Pledge Master previously[cite: 737, 785].

Taylor Durden.notes: Business senior. Denies hazing; claims Water Jeopardy was a safe, alcohol-free tradition[cite: 854, 869, 898]. Used a 'counting' system for penalties[cite: 946].
Taylor Durden.crossExam: Ignored Carmen Cordova's medical warning; increased the difficulty of the 'Pledge Olympics'[cite: 739, 954].

Dr. Ahsan Jackson.notes: Former director of the 'Body Farm'[cite: 1072]. Argues hypothyroidism and medical negligence were the true causes[cite: 1086, 1113].
Dr. Ahsan Jackson.crossExam: Paid $200k/year as an expert witness; personal bias against Dr. Chessler[cite: 1075, 1109].

Shawn Boyd.notes: Friend of Durden. Claims Jessica was 'being a clown' and could have quit[cite: 1200, 1230, 1237].
Shawn Boyd.crossExam: Close friendship with defendant; believes Alex Richards is scapegoating Durden[cite: 1200, 1257].

timeline.event: Water Jeopardy begins in ESE basement. Pledges penalized with water consumption[cite: 749, 944].
911 call placed by Alex Richards reporting Jessica Bateson is unconscious[cite: 1294, 1299].
Sgt. Knight arrives on scene; EMS transports victim to hospital[cite: 379, 1347].
Victim arrives at ER in unresponsive state with fixed/dilated pupils[cite: 1762, 1776].
Time of Death called after resuscitation efforts fail[cite: 585, 1782].
Autopsy performed; confirms brain stem swelling and distended bladder[cite: 1818, 1873, 1875].

legalElements.Manslaughter_2: Culpable mental state: Recklessly (consciously disregarding a gross risk)[cite: 304, 305].
legalElements.Hazing: Culpable mental state: Intentionally. Elements: Compelling consumption of liquid that adversely affects physical health[cite: 309, 318].
